title: Baccharis viscosissima
taxonomy: Baccharis viscosissima is classified within the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, with Baccharis viscosissima belonging to the subfamily Asteroideae. The genus Baccharis is a group of shrubs and small trees native to the Americas, and B. viscosissima is one of the species within this genus. Within the genus, B. viscosissima is often grouped with other similar species based on their shared characteristics and geographic distribution. The classification of B. viscosissima is still being refined as new research and discoveries are made.
uses: Baccharis viscosissima, also known as sticky tarweed, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's roots and leaves have been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. Its essential oil has been found to have antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent infections and promote wound healing. In traditional folk medicine, sticky tarweed has been used to treat fever, cough, and sore throats. Further research is needed to fully explore the medicinal potential of Baccharis viscosissima.

intro: Baccharis viscosissima, also known as tree baccharis or sticky baccharis, is a shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its sticky resinous leaves and small white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering relief from colds, fever, and respiratory issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Baccharis viscosissima contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The plant is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in salves and ointments for topical application, particularly for wound healing and skin issues.
